playlists.title: Python Tutorial 2026 | Complete Python Course (Beginner to Advanced) | Telusko
playlists.description:
Welcome to the Python Tutorial 2026 playlist — a complete step-by-step Python programming course designed for absolute beginners to advanced learners.
This series starts from installation and basics, and gradually takes you to functions, loops, recursion, modules, decorators, and full OOP (Object-Oriented Programming) with practical explanation.

If you want to build a strong Python foundation for Web Development, Automation, Data Science, AI/ML, or interviews — follow this playlist in order.


🔹What You’ll Learn in This Python Course:
  ✅ Python Installation & Setup (Windows/Mac/Linux basics)
  ✅ Writing Your First Python Program
  ✅ Python IDEs + IDLE + Number Operations
  ✅ Strings (Basics + Advanced)
  ✅ List, Tuple, Set, Dictionary (Core Collections)
  ✅ Variables, Data Types, Operators
  ✅ User Input using input()
  ✅ Conditions: if, else, elif, match-case
  ✅ Loops: while, for, break, continue
  ✅ Functions, Arguments, Local vs Global Variables
  ✅ Arrays + Array Operations
  ✅ Recursion + Factorial Programs
  ✅ Lambda + Higher Order Functions
  ✅ filter(), map(), reduce()
  ✅ Inner Functions + Decorators
  ✅ Modules & Packages + __name__
  ✅ OOP in Python (Classes, Objects, Constructors, Methods)
  ✅ Inheritance + super() + MRO
  ✅ Polymorphism + Duck Typing
  ✅ Operator Overloading + Method Overriding

🧠 Full Playlist Course Outline (Video-wise)
 ▶️ Basics & Setup
    1. Python Tutorial 2026 (Intro)
    2. First Code in Python
    3. Python Setup
    4. IDEs Installation
    5. Python IDLE + Number Operations
-------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ▶️ Strings & Collections
    6. Working with String
    7. More on String
    8. List in Python
    9. Tuple in List
    10. Set in Python
    11. Dictionary in Python
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ▶️ Core Fundamentals
    12. Variable Storage
    13. Data Types
    14. Operators
    15. Introduction to Functions
    16. Working with Math Module
    17. Swapping of Variables
    18. User Input (input())
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ▶️ Control Flow & Loops
    19. if in Python
    20. else + Debugging
    21. Nested if
    22. elif
    23. match-case
    24. While Loop
    25. For Loop
    26. Break & Continue
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ▶️ Functions, Arrays, Recursion
    27. Array in Python
    28. Array Functions
    29. Arguments in Function
    30. Global vs Local Variables
    31. Factorial Program
    32. Recursion
    33. Factorial using Recursion
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ▶️ Functional Programming Concepts
    34. Higher Order Function
    35. Lambda (Anonymous Function)
    36. Filter Function
    37. Map Reduce Function
    38. Inner Function
    39. Decorators
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ▶️ Modules + OOP
    40. Modules & Packages
    41. Special Variable __name__
    42. OOP Introduction
    43. Class and Object
    44. __init__ Method
    45. Constructor using __new__
    46. Types of Methods
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 ▶️ Advanced OOP
    47. Inheritance + MRO
    48. __init__ and super() with Inheritance
    49. Polymorphism + Duck Typing
    50. Operator Overloading
    51. Method Overriding
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
🎯 Who This Playlist is For
✅ Beginners starting Python from scratch
✅ Students preparing for exams / interviews
✅ Anyone learning Python for AI, ML, Data Science, Web Dev, Automation
✅ Developers switching to Python and strengthening fundamentals


Support the Channel
 If this playlist helps you, please like, share, comment, and subscribe to more tutorials.

playlists.title: Sql Tutorial for Beginners
playlists.description:
SQL Tutorial using Oracle by Suchit Khandagale
Step by Step.

SQL is a standard language for accessing and manipulating databases.SQL statements are used to perform tasks such as update data on a database, or retrieve data from a database. Some common relational database management systems that use SQL are: Oracle, Sybase, Microsoft SQL Server, Access, Ingres, etc. Although most database systems use SQL, most of them also have their own additional proprietary extensions that are usually only used on their system. However, the standard SQL commands such as "Select", "Insert", "Update", "Delete", "Create", and "Drop" can be used to accomplish almost everything that one needs to do with a database.

playlists.title: Object Cloning in Java
playlists.description:
clone (Java method) clone() is a method in the Java programming language for object duplication. In Java, objects are manipulated through reference variables, and there is no operator for copying an object—the assignment operator duplicates the reference, not the object.
- Wiki

playlists.title: MultiThreading in Java with Lambda Expression
playlists.description:
Learn Multi Threading in Java with Simple Example.
Using Thread class and Runnable Interface.

In computer science, a thread of execution is the smallest sequence of programmed instructions that can be managed independently by a scheduler, which is typically a part of the operating system.

The implementation of threads and processes differs between operating systems, but in most cases a thread is a component of a process.

Multiple threads can exist within the same process and share resources such as memory, while different processes do not share these resources.

playlists.title: Load Balancer Essentials for System Design Interviews (Java Brains)
playlists.description:
Master load balancing inside out and ace your system design interviews. Build a rock‑solid understanding of every essential load‑balancing concept, technique and algorithm, giving you the edge in any system design whiteboard challenge.

Why Load Balancing Matters
Load balancing isn’t optional! It’s the backbone of every production‑grade system. Interviewers will expect you to speak fluently about latency vs. throughput, session affinity, multi‑layer architectures, and the trade‑offs of static, dynamic, and hybrid algorithms.

In this course, you’ll learn how each load‑balancing technique works. Packed with the real concepts, diagrams, and all the decision‑frameworks you need to stand out on your system design interview.

playlists.title: LLM Concepts Deep Dive (Official Course Playlist)
playlists.description:
LLM Concepts Deep Dive: Conceptual Mastery for Developers

Access the full course by becoming a member: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCYt1sfh5464XaDBH0oH_o7Q/join
Same content but with discounted price on the website: 
https://www.javabrains.io/bundles/all-access-subscription

---

About the course

The Developer’s Guide to LLM Internals

Master transformers, embeddings, and RAG. Learn the internals of modern AI and understand practical solutions with vector databases.

Beneath the surface of every ChatGPT response lies a sophisticated architecture few truly understand. Anyone can send a prompt, but only those who grasp tokenization, embedding spaces, transformer attention, and retrieval-augmented generation can build robust AI-powered solutions.

This course deep dives into these concepts, transforming theory into practical knowledge you can implement immediately. From the mathematics of vector embeddings to the pipelines that power Retrieval-Augmented Generation, you’ll gain the skills top AI companies look for in technical interviews.

Curriculum highlights:

1. Language Modeling & Training – Understand autoencoding vs. autoregressive models and text completion workflows.
2. Training Methodologies – Explore pre-training, instruction tuning, and fine-tuning strategies.
3. Tokens & Embeddings – See how token boundaries form, visualize embeddings in N-dimensional space, and work through embedding math.
4. Knowledge Assessment & Semantic Similarity – Test your understanding and learn how text similarity underpins search and retrieval.
5. Transformer Architecture – Break down attention mechanisms and the flow from tokens to text.
6. Context Management – Manage context windows effectively and handle stateless interactions.
7. RAG & Vector Databases – Build full Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipelines with pseudocode examples and Q&A.

Course details:

* 36 lessons
* 3 hours of deep-dive video content

playlists.title: Java to AI - The Python-Free Guide to Large Language Models
playlists.description:
A complete introduction to the exciting world of Large Language Models, specifically designed for Java developers who want to understand and implement AI without needing Python.

Introduction to Core LLM concepts
A Complete Code-Free Guide

Feel left behind by the AI revolution because you don't know Python? This course is your gateway to the exciting world of Large Language Models, specifically designed for Java developers who want to understand and implement AI without changing their tech stack.

Through clear, jargon-free explanations, you'll discover how LLMs actually work - from their fundamental architecture and tokenization to sophisticated prompting techniques and integration patterns. We explore everything from the basics of what AI truly is to advanced concepts like context windows, structured outputs, and error handling strategies.

The curriculum methodically builds your knowledge: starting with AI and LLM foundations, moving to practical Java integration with HTTP, then advancing to conversation management, prompting patterns, and robust error handling. Each concept is explained in plain English with practical insights.

playlists.title: The Big Picture - Java Backend Bootcamp
A Java Developer Learns Python From Scratch - Completely Live
playlists.description:
Join me on this exciting journey as an experienced Java developer embarks on a thrilling quest to learn Python from scratch, all captured in a series of lively and engaging live videos. Get ready to witness the magic of learning unfold, as I fearlessly steps into the world of Python, armed with curiosity, determination, and a whole lot of fun!

In this captivating playlist, you'll accompany me through each step of the learning process, from the basics to more advanced concepts, all in real-time. You'll experience the joy of discovery as we tackle Python's syntax, data structures, control flow, and much more. Expect plenty of "aha!" moments and occasional humorous bloopers along the way, as we embrace the learning process with laughter and excitement.

This playlist is not your typical dry tutorial. Instead, it's a vibrant showcase of interactive coding sessions, playful experimentation, and occasional surprises to keep you thoroughly entertained. As I explore Python's unique features, I'll also share insights, tips, and tricks gained from my Java background, creating a dynamic bridge between the two programming languages.

playlists.title: Claude 3.5 Sonnet: Computer use
playlists.description:
With the upgraded Claude 3.5 Sonnet, we’re introducing a new capability in beta: computer use. Developers can now direct Claude to use computers the way people do—by looking at a screen, moving a cursor, clicking, and typing text.

While groundbreaking, computer use is still experimental—at times cumbersome and error-prone. We're releasing computer use early for feedback from developers.
